summary_one_line: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte FOURCC name equals the literal 'tRNS' (74 52 4E 53) must appear so the linear chunk-name comparison chain reaches the tRNS arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must contain a PNG chunk whose 4-byte name field exactly equals the FOURCC literal 'tRNS' (bytes 74 52 4E 53) so the `chunk_name == png_tRNS` equality at pngread.c:244 evaluates true. chunk_name is built from a fixed-offset 4-byte field in a chunk header, and the dispatch is a linear `else if (chunk_name == png_X)` ladder that compares against each registered FOURCC constant.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and hits the true side (avg hitcount 276). Its winning seeds carry the structured PNG chunk layout (PNG magic + IHDR + named chunks like tEXt/gAMA/sRGB at the 4-byte chunk-name offsets), and the I2S-floor dash-row ancestor confirms an input-to-state substitution event produced the exact-match FOURCC bytes the equality demands.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks in 10/10 trials with 0 hits on the true side. At line 244 the loser shows T=0 F=24 — it walks the whole comparison ladder and never matches the tRNS constant; its mutated chunk-name bytes (e.g. 'tRGB' at 0x35-0x38, 'gAMA' at 0x25-0x28) never randomly land the exact 4-byte 74 52 4E 53 needle that the equality requires.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recorded when `chunk_name == png_tRNS` executes, sees the constant operand (the tRNS FOURCC), scans the input for the other operand's byte sequence, and substitutes the constant at a chunk-name offset — directly synthesizing the 4-byte exact-match that naive's blind havoc cannot brute-force in a 32-bit equality.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the equality chunk_name == png_tRNS at pngread.c:244, true side globally blocked.
chunk_name is read from a fixed 4-byte field of each chunk header inside a linear comparison ladder.
cmplog resolves and hits the true side in all trials while naive never does.
The loser never takes the tRNS true side; it walks the ladder with zero true hits.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 244  T=12 F=14  T=0 F=24  else if (chunk_name == png_tRNS)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning cmplog seeds carry structured PNG chunk layout with named 4-byte chunk fields.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S facility, only blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3891 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 03c147a0a4ec14e2` and `--seed 016607d5919b9103`. Both chains contain the same I2S-floor dash row ancestor 94c47bff5687a0c9 (mutation_op = -): at depth 5 for the first seed and depth 18 for the second. The tool's trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line reports 1 dash row in each chain — direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace contribution to the winner.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte exact-equality FOURCC where cmplog and naive resolve the branch at statistically indistinguishable rates (no I2S advantage), e.g. naive reaching the true side in >=8/10 trials within the same budget.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff does not show a winner offset where bytes 74 52 4E 53 ('tRNS') appear verbatim in the 5 displayed seeds, so the exact placement of the I2S-substituted tRNS FOURCC is inferred from the equality semantics rather than directly observed in a shown seed.
